steps.title: Use the slope to find k
steps.content:
The points $(3,7)$ and $(k,11)$ are on a line with slope $2$, so
$$\frac{11-7}{k-3}=2$$
$$\frac{4}{k-3}=2$$
$$4=2(k-3)$$
$$k=5$$

steps.title: Use the slope to find n
steps.content:
The points $(3,7)$ and $(12,n)$ are also on the line, so
$$\frac{n-7}{12-3}=2$$
$$\frac{n-7}{9}=2$$
$$n-7=18$$
$$n=25$$

steps.title: Add the constants
steps.content:
Now add the two values:
$$k+n=5+25=30$$
The answer is 30.
